---
title: "LRP66: Do letter-sounds and blending act as independent phonics paths to word reading? (two-mediator decomposition)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

The route-composite [LRP62](../lrp-rli-med-062) bundles letter-sound knowledge $L$ and
phoneme blending $B$ into a single code-based route and cannot say how much runs through
blending *over and above* letter sounds. LRP66 is the phonics analogue of
[LRP64](../lrp-rli-med-064) (which split $L$ vs expressive vocabulary): a **two-mediator**
decomposition of the word-reading ($W$) effect into a letter-sound path $L$, a
blending path $B$, and a direct/residual path.

We report the **joint indirect effect** through the $\{L, B\}$ block (the robust headline)
plus a clearly-flagged **exploratory** path split $\text{NIE}_L$ / $\text{NIE}_B$.

## Design and causal structure

Phase 0 only (`phase_mode="itt"`, t1 → t2), $n \approx 53$, one row per child ($G = 1$
intervention, $G = 0$ wait-list). Two count mediators $L_{t2}$ and $B_{t2}$ (Beta-Binomial
legs on their own baselines); outcome $W_{t2}$ with $G\times L$ and $G\times B$
interactions. Adjustment $\{G, A, E, R, W_\text{pre}, L_{t1}, B_{t1}\}$ — the two
vocabulary measures $E, R$ are the measured mediator–outcome confounders of the
code-route → reading path, at baseline (cross-world assumption).

## Assumptions — read this first {#sec-assumptions}

A **decomposition under assumptions, not proof of a causal route.** Randomisation secures
$G\to Y$ and $G\to M$; the binding, unverifiable assumption is **no unmeasured
mediator–outcome confounding** — the revised DAG (ID-2) names latent general ability $GA$
and treatment-induced dose as the threats, so every quantity here is an **adjusted
association**, not point-identified. $L$ and $B$ are strongly correlated phonics skills, so
read the per-path split with particular caution. Contemporaneous measurement; sight-reading;
$n \approx 53 \Rightarrow$ wide intervals.

{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
